title: Proficiency System
description: Training and expertise mechanics that enhance character capabilities

overview.description: Characters and monsters are good at various things. Some are skilled with many weapons, while others can use only a few. Some are better at understanding people's motives, and others are better at unlocking the secrets of the multiverse.
overview.universality: All creatures have a Proficiency Bonus, which reflects the impact that training has on the creature's capabilities.

proficiency_bonus.description: This bonus is applied to a D20 Test when the creature has proficiency in a skill, in a saving throw, or with an item that the creature uses to make the D20 Test. The bonus is also used for spell attacks and for calculating the DC of saving throws for spells.

determination.characters: A character's Proficiency Bonus increases as the character gains levels
determination.monsters: A monster's Proficiency Bonus is based on its Challenge Rating

stacking_rules.no_stacking: Your Proficiency Bonus can't be added to a die roll or another number more than once
stacking_rules.example: If a rule allows you to make a Charisma (Deception or Persuasion) check, you add your Proficiency Bonus if you're proficient in either skill, but you don't add it twice if you're proficient in both skills
stacking_rules.multiplication: Occasionally, a Proficiency Bonus might be multiplied or divided (doubled or halved, for example) before being added
stacking_rules.expertise_example: The Expertise feature doubles the Proficiency Bonus for certain ability checks
stacking_rules.modification_limits: Whenever the bonus is used, it can be multiplied only once and divided only once

skill_proficiencies.overview: Most ability checks involve using a skill, which represents a category of things creatures try to do with an ability check

application.with_proficiency: If a creature is proficient in a skill, the creature applies its Proficiency Bonus to ability checks involving that skill
application.without_proficiency: Without proficiency in a skill, a creature can still make ability checks involving that skill but doesn't add its Proficiency Bonus

skill_proficiencies.example: If a character tries to climb a cliff, the DM might ask for a Strength (Athletics) check. If the character has Athletics proficiency, the character adds their Proficiency Bonus to the Strength check. If the character lacks that proficiency, they make the check without adding their Proficiency Bonus.
skill_proficiencies.dm_authority: The DM has the ultimate say on whether a skill is relevant in a situation
skill_proficiencies.determination: A character's starting skill proficiencies are determined at character creation, and a monster's skill proficiencies appear in its stat block

different_abilities.description: Each skill proficiency is associated with an ability check. In some situations, the DM might allow you to apply your skill proficiency to a different ability check
different_abilities.example: If a character tries to intimidate someone through a show of physical strength, the DM might ask for a Strength (Intimidation) check rather than a Charisma (Intimidation) check. That character would make a Strength check and add their Proficiency Bonus if they have Intimidation proficiency.

saving_throw_proficiencies.description: Proficiency in a saving throw lets a character add their Proficiency Bonus to saves that use a particular ability
saving_throw_proficiencies.example: Proficiency in Wisdom saves lets you add your Proficiency Bonus to your Wisdom saves
saving_throw_proficiencies.monsters: Some monsters also have saving throw proficiencies, as noted in their stat blocks
saving_throw_proficiencies.class_training: Each class gives proficiency in at least two saving throws, representing that class's training in evading or resisting certain threats
saving_throw_proficiencies.wizard_example: Wizards, for example, are proficient in Intelligence and Wisdom saves; they train to resist mental assault

equipment_proficiencies.description: A character gains proficiency with various weapons and tools from their class and background

weapons.description: Anyone can wield a weapon, but proficiency makes you better at wielding it
weapons.benefit: If you have proficiency with a weapon, you add your Proficiency Bonus to attack rolls you make with it

tools.description: If you have proficiency with a tool, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make that uses the tool
tools.skill_synergy: If you have proficiency in the skill that's also used with that check, you have Advantage on the check too
tools.double_benefit: This means you can benefit from both skill proficiency and tool proficiency on the same ability check

proficiency_types.description: 18 different skills covering various activities and knowledge areas
proficiency_types.application: Added to ability checks when relevant skill proficiency applies

proficiency_types.description: Proficiency in one or more of the six ability-based saves
proficiency_types.application: Added to saving throws of the relevant ability type

proficiency_types.description: Proficiency with specific weapons or weapon groups
proficiency_types.application: Added to attack rolls made with those weapons

proficiency_types.description: Proficiency with various tools and kits
proficiency_types.application: Added to ability checks made using those tools

proficiency_types.description: Ability to speak, read, and write languages
proficiency_types.application: No mechanical bonus, but enables communication and understanding
